examples.title: Measuring a Room
examples.content: You measure a room. Length = 5 meters. Width = 4 meters. These are scalars—just numbers. But if you say the door is 5 meters north, that's a vector—number + direction. Both are physical quantities.

examples.title: Speed vs Velocity
examples.content: You drive 60 km/h. That's speed (scalar). You drive 60 km/h north. That's velocity (vector). The difference? Direction matters. Velocity tells you both how fast and which way.

examples.title: Force with Direction
examples.content: Pushing with 100 N of force is different if you push up vs down. Same number (100 N), different effect. That's why force is a vector—direction matters!
